{{Infobox character
| name = Indirial
| appears_in = The Traveler's Gate Trilogy
| titles = Overlord, Overlord of the realm of Cana, bearer of the first Dragon's Fang
| abilities = make his sword vanish, cut open a Valinhall Gate, unorthodox application of a lesser-known Valinhall power
| affiliations = Valinhall Traveler, Tartarus group
| introduced = House of Blades
}}

''See also: [[Indirial Timeline]]''

== Overview ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|Indirial is the [[Overlord of Cana]] and a highly trusted, long-standing servant to the [[King]]. Though he holds significant authority that places him second only to the monarch, he still maintains a respectful manner by bowing to royalty. Physically, he appears to be in his late thirties or early forties despite being in his fifties, possessing tanned skin, dark eyes, and short dark hair. He is known for wearing shirts with the sleeves removed to display black chain tattoos that wrap around his arms, and he carries a gold medallion featuring a dark amethyst. As a formidable warrior, he is described as being much stronger than an average person and possesses the capability to carry out any execution ordered by his [[King]] without hesitation. He is characterized by a cheerful and vigilant personality, often greeting others with a grin.}}

== House of Blades ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Indirial]] is someone who would kill [[Kai]] and [[Denner]] if they tried anything.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c11">''House of Blades'', chapter 11</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Indirial]] is the oldest confidant and most trusted servant of [[Leah's father]].<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c23">''House of Blades'', chapter 23</ref>}}

== The Crimson Vault ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|An [[Overlord]] of the realm of [[Cana]] and a [[Valinhall]] [[Traveler]], Indirial is the son of [[Kalman]] and Aleias who serves under His Majesty [[Zakareth the Sixth]]. Clad in a black [[Nye cloak]] with black chain tattoos crawling up his forearms, he wields a chipped and pitted Dragon’s [[Fang]] named [[Vasha]] that he can make vanish. Throughout his service, he has fought alongside the [[Damascans]] against [[Valin]], saved [[Kai]] by using a healing pool, and provided information to [[Leah]]. While managing emergencies at the camp and discussing strategy with [[Captain]] [[Erastes]], he also commands the [[Tartarus]] group. After a period of being missing, he eventually provided [[Simon]] with assurances regarding [[Naraka]] and responded to a signal for help from [[Leah]].}}

{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Indirial]] is a character who wears a black [[Nye cloak]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c1">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 1</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Indirial]] has a daughter named [[Elaina]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c1" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Indirial]]'s parents were cut apart by [[Damascan soldiers]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c1" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Indirial]] wears a [[Nye cloak]] and has chains on his arms.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c1" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Indirial]] possesses a Dragon’s [[Fang]] named [[Vasha]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c1" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Indirial]] can make his [[sword]] vanish.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c1" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Indirial]] possesses a [[diamond]] that provides clarity and simplicity.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c1" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Indirial]] believes the [[Enosh Travelers]]' success in destroying the [[Tree]] would make no one safe.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c1" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Indirial]] is an [[Overlord]] who has been a fixture around the royal palace.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c3">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 3</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Indirial]] is an [[Overlord]] and a [[Valinhall]] [[Traveler]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c3" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Indirial]] has black chain tattoos on his arms.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c3" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|Black chains crawl up [[Indirial]]'s forearms.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c3" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Indirial]] once saved [[Kai]] by dragging him to the healing pool.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c4">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 4</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Indirial]] is someone [[Kai]] intends to alert.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c4" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Indirial]] fought [[Valin]] and stood with the [[Damascans]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c4" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Indirial]] is a character who sprints through the halls of [[Cana]]'s royal palace.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c4" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Indirial]] provided information to [[Leah]] about [[Simon]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c5">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 5</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Indirial]] might be able to lure the [[Incarnation]] back into his home [[Territory]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c8">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 8</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Indirial]] is a person associated with [[Kathrin]] and the old master.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c9">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 9</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Indirial]] is a [[Valinhall]] [[Traveler]] with dark hair and dark skin who has black chain-marks on his arms.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c9" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Indirial]] is an [[Overlord]] of the realm of [[Cana]] and bearer of the first [[Dragon's Fang]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c9" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Indirial]] uses a chipped and pitted [[Dragon's Fang]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c9" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Simon]] had previously watched a battle involving [[Indirial]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c10">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 10</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Simon]] is stalling until [[Indirial]]'s reinforcements arrive.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c12">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 12</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Indirial]] possesses a medallion with a tiny dark gem at its center.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c15">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 15</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Indirial]] is the son of Aleias.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c15" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Indirial]] is the [[Overlord]] of the realm of [[Cana]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c15" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Indirial]] is a subject to the authority of His Majesty [[Zakareth the Sixth]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c15" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Indirial]] is the son of [[Kalman]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c15" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Indirial]] is an [[Overlord]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c15" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Indirial]] offered [[Leah]] sympathy when her mother died.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c15" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Indirial]] has been missing.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c16">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 16</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Indirial]] assured [[Simon]] that [[Naraka]] is the fastest and most reliable way to Travel between set points.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c17">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 17</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Indirial]] challenged [[Valin]] after seeing through his insanity.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c18">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 18</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Indirial]] fought [[Kai]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c18" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Indirial]] worked for the [[King]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c18" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Indirial]] is a person who handles emergencies and puts out fires around the camp.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c20">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 20</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Leah]] signaled [[Indirial]] for help.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c21">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 21</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Indirial]] commands the [[Tartarus]] group.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c21" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Indirial]] is a [[Valinhall]] [[Traveler]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c21" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Indirial]] cleared his throat.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c21" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Indirial]] held a small box for [[Leah]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c23">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 23</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Indirial]] is someone [[Simon]] hopes will be home.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c25">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 25</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Indirial]] was talking strategy with a captain named [[Erastes]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c25" />}}

== City of Light ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|An [[Overlord]] and the son of Aleias, Indirial is a [[Valinhall]] [[Traveler]] marked by shadowy chains on his forearms and a spiked black gauntlet known as a [[Valinhall gatecrawler]]. Hired by [[Leah's father]] to guard the ruling monarch, he uses a long, cracked [[sword]] and possesses the ability to cut open [[Valinhall]] [[Gates]]. During the conflict against [[Alin]]'s army, he utilized unorthodox powers and translucent green light armor before being found near death while attempting to cut a Gate. After being suspended in a prison of black ice, he eventually appeared alongside [[King]] [[Zakareth]] with his arm chains turned to [[steel]] and a cloak of shadows. Ultimately, Indirial became the corrupted [[Valinhall Incarnation]] after attacking the [[House of Blades]], leading to him being sealed within his own [[Territory]] where he manages his realm from [[Valinhall]].}}

{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Zakareth]] wants [[Indirial]] on his side.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c2">''City of Light'', chapter 2</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Indirial]]'s forearms are marked with shadowy chains.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c3">''City of Light'', chapter 3</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Indirial]]'s left hand is covered by a [[Valinhall gatecrawler]], which is a spiked black gauntlet.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c4">''City of Light'', chapter 4</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Indirial]] is a person [[Valin]] reminds [[Simon]] of.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c6">''City of Light'', chapter 6</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Indirial]] said he needed [[Simon]]'s help.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c6" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Indirial]] previously saved [[Simon]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c6" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Indirial]] is an [[Overlord]] with black chain marks on his forearms.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c7">''City of Light'', chapter 7</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Indirial]] was hired by [[Leah's father]] to guard the ruling monarch.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c7" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Indirial]] uses a long, cracked [[sword]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c7" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Indirial]] has the ability to cut open a [[Valinhall Gate]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c7" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Indirial]] spent better than an hour inside [[Valinhall]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c9">''City of Light'', chapter 9</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Indirial]] uses an unorthodox application of a lesser-known [[Valinhall]] power.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c9" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Indirial]] finished a discussion with Feiora.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c10">''City of Light'', chapter 10</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Indirial]] shouted warnings to people to get back.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c11">''City of Light'', chapter 11</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Indirial]] is a [[Valinhall]] [[Traveler]] and the son of Aleias.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c11" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Indirial]] is part of the group taking out [[Alin]]'s army.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c11" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Indirial]] has a grip like an [[Ornheim golem]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c12">''City of Light'', chapter 12</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Indirial]] can wear a plate of translucent green light like armor.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c12" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Indirial]] was found sprawled on the steps of a waystation.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c13">''City of Light'', chapter 13</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Indirial]] is an [[Overlord]] who can call a flickering version of ghost armor.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c13" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Indirial]] is attempting to cut a Gate.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c14">''City of Light'', chapter 14</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Indirial]] is near death.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c14" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Lycus]] wants to help [[Indirial]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c14" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Indirial]] is a person the [[Eldest]] considered for the [[Nye essence]] instead of [[Simon]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c16">''City of Light'', chapter 16</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Indirial]] would likely bring everything to his [[Queen]] first.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c16" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Indirial]] was suspended in a prison of black ice.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c17">''City of Light'', chapter 17</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Indirial]] uses [[liquid steel]] from [[Valinhall]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c17" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Zakareth]] tells [[Indirial]] that if he does not survive, others will not either.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c17" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Indirial]] called stone.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c17" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Indirial]] is a voice of caution in [[Leah]]'s mind.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c18">''City of Light'', chapter 18</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Indirial]]'s wife and daughter have also been denied water.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c19">''City of Light'', chapter 19</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Indirial]] can call upon [[steel]] to keep himself on his feet.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c19" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Indirial]] wields a straight [[sword]] in his left hand and a [[Dragon's Fang]] in his right.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c20">''City of Light'', chapter 20</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Indirial]]'s focus is filtered by the [[Valinhall]] [[diamond]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c20" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Indirial]] possesses a weapon named [[Vasha]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c20" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Indirial]]'s presence hangs over others like a banner.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c20" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Indirial]] has succumbed to his chains.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c20" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Indirial]] is a powerful avatar of [[the House]] wandering in the outside world.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c20" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Indirial]] was last seen in the camp five days ago.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c21">''City of Light'', chapter 21</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Indirial]] stands next to [[King]] [[Zakareth]] with chains on his arms turned to [[steel]] and a cloak of shadows.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c21" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Indirial]] is a warrior and a trusted servant, more than just an [[Incarnation]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c21" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Indirial]] represents the [[Incarnation of Ragnarus]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c21" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Indirial]] felt [[Kai]]'s rage and the destruction of [[Tartarus]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c22">''City of Light'', chapter 22</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Indirial]] is the [[Valinhall Incarnation]] [[Kai]] encountered on the tiles of the [[House of Blades]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c22" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Indirial]] is the first student of [[Kai]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c22" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Leah]] suspects the [[King]] may have taken [[Indirial]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c22" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Indirial]] is an [[Incarnation]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c23">''City of Light'', chapter 23</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Indirial]] has the ability to summon weapons from the [[Valinhall armory]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c23" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Indirial]] possesses [[Nye essence]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c23" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Indirial]] possesses a [[Territory]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c23" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Indirial]] is the [[Valinhall Incarnation]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c23" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Indirial]] is a shadow-cloaked [[Incarnation]] sitting in the entry hall.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c24">''City of Light'', chapter 24</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Indirial]] has violet eyes.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c24" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Indirial]] has been sealed into the [[Territory]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c24" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Indirial]] became an [[Incarnation]] after fighting another [[Valinhall]] [[Traveler]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c25">''City of Light'', chapter 25</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Indirial]] has been corrupted.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c25" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Indirial]] attacked the [[House of Blades]] and was sealed in [[the House]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c25" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Indirial]] was forced to embrace [[Valinhall]] and become an [[Incarnation]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c26">''City of Light'', chapter 26</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Leah]] has [[Indirial]] with her.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c27">''City of Light'', chapter 27</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Zakareth]] mentions [[Indirial]]'s job.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c27" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Indirial]] oversees the day-to-day management of his realm from within [[Valinhall]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c28">''City of Light'', chapter 28</ref>}}

== Notable quotes ==
* {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|“And, if we tried anything, you know Indirial would kill us both.”<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c11" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|“Indirial, he…he fought me. He sealed me.” Valin’s face contorted with rage. “He stood with the Damascans.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c4" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|"Perhaps Indirial can even lure this Incarnation back into his home Territory."<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c8" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|"The Overlord responded, "but there's no point."<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c15" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|“And the Valinhall Traveler, Indirial, son of Aleias.”<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c11" />}}

== Notes ==
<references />

{{Traveler's Gate nav}}

[[Category:Characters]]
[[Category:House of Blades]]
[[Category:The Crimson Vault]]
[[Category:City of Light]]
[[Category:Traveler's Gate]]
[[Category:Traveler's Gate characters]]
[[Category:Travelers]]
[[Category:Viewpoint characters]]
